metadata_details.purpose: Parser for microsoft logs migrated from samples-main
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of microsoft logs in appropriate format
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

analysis.semantic_summary: A simple parser designed to process Microsoft Active Directory logs using a Gron format pattern matching. It extracts key-value pairs enclosed in square brackets, primarily focused on authentication and directory service events.
optimization_opportunities: Add specific field extractions beyond basic pattern
Include validation for expected AD field names
Implement structured field mapping to OCSF
Add error handling for malformed entries
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: microsoft_activedirectory_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Authentication (3002)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:09:14.787814

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local BRACKET_PATTERN = "%[%s*(.-)%s*=%s*(.-)%s*%]"

-- Local cache for common string operations
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format
local table_insert = table.insert

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output structure with local references
    local output = {
        class_uid = 3002,
        class_name = "Authentication", 
        category_uid = 3,
        category_name = "Identity & Access Management",
        metadata = {
            version = "1.0.0",
            product = {
                name = "Microsoft Active Directory",
                vendor_name = "Microsoft"
            }
        },
        time = os.time() * 1000
    }

    -- Extract and process bracket-enclosed pairs
    local raw_event = {}
    for line in pairs(record) do
        local key, value = string_match(line, BRACKET_PATTERN)
        if key and value then
            -- Sanitize and validate extracted values
            key = string.gsub(key, "[^%w_]", "_")
            raw_event[key] = value
        end
    end

    -- Store raw event data in metadata
    output.metadata.raw_event = raw_event

    -- Extract common AD fields if present
    local event_type = raw_event.event_type
    if event_type then
        output.type = string_format("AD_%s", string.upper(event_type))
    end

    -- Validate required OCSF fields
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    -- Add observability metadata
    output.metadata.parser_version = "latest"
    output.metadata.processing_time = os.time() * 1000 - output.time

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

-- Validation helper functions
local function validate_field(value, field_type)
    if field_type == "string" then
        return type(value) == "string" and value ~= ""
    elseif field_type == "number" then
        return type(value) == "number"
    end
    return false
end

-- Test cases
local function run_tests()
    -- Test 1: Valid input
    local test1 = {
        "[event_type = login]",
        "[user = admin]",
        "[status = success]"
    }
    local result1 = safe_transform(test1)
    assert(result1.class_uid == 3002)

    -- Test 2: Invalid input
    local result2 = safe_transform(nil)
    assert(result2 == nil)

    -- Test 3: Malformed data
    local test3 = {
        "invalid data",
        "[partial"
    }
    local result3 = safe_transform(test3)
    assert(result3 ~= nil)
end

deployment_notes.monitoring_setup: Enable detailed logging for first 24 hours
